title: The Last Temptation Of Homer

jokes.text: D'oh! [Screams]
jokes.setup: Homer crashes his car in the opening sequence
jokes.punchline: His signature frustrated exclamation
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer catchphrase that defines the character. Perfect economy and integration.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: D'oh!

jokes.text: The beauty of it is each parking space is a mere one foot narrower-- indistinguishable to the naked eye... but therein lies the game.
jokes.setup: Bart explains his parking lot sabotage scheme
jokes.punchline: His gleeful, sophisticated description of causing chaos
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bart character comedy - sophisticated evil in a child. The formal language contrasts brilliantly with the petty chaos he's causing.

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: I strategically placed banana peels in premium parking spaces, short-circuited the ticket machine, and released a swarm of bees in the attendant's booth. It's not just chaos, man—it's *organized* chaos with a touch of class.

jokes.text: I fear to watch, yet I cannot turn away.
jokes.setup: Watching the parking lot chaos unfold
jokes.punchline: Ned's overly dramatic, literary response to a parking situation
jokes.explanation: Classic Ned - turning mundane situations into overly dramatic observations with highbrow language.

dialogue.speaker: Ned Flanders
dialogue.line: Well, I declare! This parking lot has become a veritable Shakespearean tragedy of automotive proportions. Verily, the lack of orderly vehicle placement is as troubling as a Dickensian workhouse! Diddily-doodily, somebody oughta call the authorities-diddily-diddily!

jokes.text: [ Man ] I can't get out! [ Woman ] I'm trapped. [ Man ] I can't breathe!
jokes.setup: Parking lot chaos from narrow spaces
jokes.punchline: Drivers becoming increasingly panicked and dramatic
jokes.explanation: Good escalation from inconvenience to existential panic. Shows Burns' scheme working perfectly.

dialogue.speaker: Driver 1
dialogue.line: Come on, just pull in already!

dialogue.speaker: Driver 2
dialogue.line: I'm trying! There's like two inches on each side!

dialogue.speaker: Driver 3
dialogue.line: Move it! Some of us have places to be!

dialogue.speaker: Driver 2
dialogue.line: Oh, like where? The unemployment line?

dialogue.speaker: Driver 1
dialogue.line: That's it, I'm going in!

dialogue.speaker: Driver 4
dialogue.line: No, I saw this spot first!

dialogue.speaker: Driver 3
dialogue.line: This is madness! Absolute madness!

dialogue.speaker: Driver 2
dialogue.line: I'm abandoning my car. I'm just going to walk.

dialogue.speaker: Driver 1
dialogue.line: We're all walking!

jokes.text: Bart laughing sinisterly
jokes.setup: The parking lot chaos
jokes.punchline: Revealing Bart caused it all and is enjoying the mayhem
jokes.explanation: Classic Bart behavior - causing chaos and delighting in the results

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: Eat my shorts, man. This is the best day ever!

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Why is the parking lot a complete disaster?

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: I may have let the air out of everyone's tires.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Why would you do that?!

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: Because it's hilarious, dude.

jokes.text: God bless America. But my classroom is not a democracy. Ha!
jokes.setup: Teacher talks about American democracy and innocent until proven guilty
jokes.punchline: Immediately contradicts herself with authoritarian classroom rule
jokes.explanation: Sharp political satire about authoritarianism disguised as patriotism. The immediate contradiction is perfectly structured.

dialogue.speaker: Mrs. Krabappel
dialogue.line: In America, we believe in democracy and the principle of innocent until proven guilty.

dialogue.speaker: Mrs. Krabappel
dialogue.line: Now sit down and shut up, or I'll send you to the principal's office!

jokes.text: Uh-- Um-- Hmm? Uh-- [Grunting] Mmm-- Uh--
jokes.setup: Bart is called on to read a word he can't see
jokes.punchline: His extended, painful struggling with unintelligible sounds
jokes.explanation: Extended cringe comedy that builds the setup for the vision problem revelation.

dialogue.speaker: Teacher
dialogue.line: Bart, can you read the next word?

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: Uh... it's... guh... gluh... gwuh...

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: Bleurgh... schleeeuh...

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: Thrrrugh... mwaaaahhh...

jokes.text: It's 'photosynthesis'! Damn your feeble brain!
jokes.setup: Martin eagerly volunteers to answer when Bart can't
jokes.punchline: His sudden vicious insult after being ignored
jokes.explanation: Perfect Martin character moment - the good student's frustration boiling over into unexpected hostility.

dialogue.speaker: Martin
dialogue.line: Ooh! Ooh! I know! I know! Pick me!

dialogue.speaker: Martin
dialogue.line: You four-eyed dweeb! You pathetic loser!

jokes.text: You mean it ain't me noggin', it's me peepers? Oh, well, that's just 'loverly.'
jokes.setup: Doctor suggests Bart's problems might be vision-related
jokes.punchline: Bart's sudden Cockney accent and 'My Fair Lady' reference
jokes.explanation: Unexpected cultural reference delivered in perfect Cockney accent. The randomness works because it's so confidently executed.

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: Well blow me down, gov'nor! I'm a Cockney now, innit? I could be the next Professor 'Iggins!

jokes.text: Hey, this emergency exit is painted on! There's no way out! There's no way out! We're doomed! Doomed!
jokes.setup: Workers try to escape through emergency exit during plant accident
jokes.punchline: The exit is fake, leading to panic about being trapped
jokes.explanation: Dark commentary on workplace safety violations. The escalating panic is both funny and disturbing.

dialogue.speaker: Worker 1
dialogue.line: The emergency exit! Come on!

dialogue.speaker: Worker 2
dialogue.line: It's painted on!

dialogue.speaker: Worker 1
dialogue.line: What?!

dialogue.speaker: Worker 2
dialogue.line: It's just a picture of a door!

dialogue.speaker: Multiple Workers
dialogue.line: Nooooo!

jokes.text: How about real lead in the radiation shields? Urinal cakes, maybe?
jokes.setup: Burns asks what else they'd like for safety improvements
jokes.punchline: Homer's sarcastic suggestions reveal more safety violations
jokes.explanation: Homer's rare moment of awareness about workplace conditions, delivered with perfect sarcasm.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Well, Mr. Burns, how about a bucket of ice water for my groin?

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Or maybe some asbestos padding for my desk chair?

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Oh! And those radioactive donuts in the break room - could we maybe label those?

jokes.text: Smithers, where does that tube go? I'm not sure, sir. It was here when we moved in.
jokes.setup: Burns throws Homer into a mysterious tube
jokes.punchline: They don't know where it leads - it was just there
jokes.explanation: Perfectly absurd corporate negligence. The casual acceptance of mystery infrastructure is wonderfully absurd.

dialogue.speaker: Mr. Burns
dialogue.line: Release the hounds!

dialogue.speaker: Smithers
dialogue.line: Sir, where does this tube lead?

dialogue.speaker: Mr. Burns
dialogue.line: I haven't the faintest idea. It's been there for years.

jokes.text: [ Men ] Dance! Dance! Dance! Dance! [ Men Ululating ]
jokes.setup: Employee shot through mystery tube
jokes.punchline: He lands in what appears to be an exotic dance club or harem
jokes.explanation: Completely unexpected and surreal payoff to the mystery tube. The ululating makes it perfectly exotic and random.

dialogue.speaker: Homer Simpson
dialogue.line: Woohoo! Best job ever!

dialogue.speaker: Lenny
dialogue.line: Dude, where'd he go?

dialogue.speaker: Carl
dialogue.line: Down the tube, man.

jokes.text: Excellent, Zutroy. Work hard and each day you'll get a shiny penny.
jokes.setup: Burns needs a new worker for dangerous job
jokes.punchline: He pays an illegal immigrant one penny per day
jokes.explanation: Dark satire of exploitative labor practices. Burns' cheerful delivery of the exploitation is perfectly in character.

dialogue.speaker: Mr. Burns
dialogue.line: I need a new worker for this dangerous job. How much would an illegal immigrant work for?

jokes.text: That's preposterous. Zutroy here is as American as apple pie. [Foreign Language] Mr. Burns.
jokes.setup: Burns denies Zutroy is an illegal alien
jokes.punchline: Zutroy immediately speaks in foreign language
jokes.explanation: Perfect timing on the contradiction. Burns' confident lie immediately undermined.

dialogue.speaker: Mr. Burns
dialogue.line: You're not an illegal alien, Zutroy.

dialogue.speaker: Zutroy
dialogue.line: ¡Gracias, Señor Burns! ¡Usted es muy amable!

jokes.text: [ Foreign Language ] Mr. Burns.
jokes.setup: Burns claiming Zutroy is American
jokes.punchline: Zutroy immediately speaks in foreign language
jokes.explanation: Perfect immediate contradiction of Burns' lie. Economy is excellent - no translation needed, the timing says everything.

dialogue.speaker: Mr. Burns
dialogue.line: You're American, aren't you?

dialogue.speaker: Zutroy
dialogue.line: Si, si, señor!

jokes.text: We found a missing Brazilian soccer team working in your reactor core. That plane crashed on my property!
jokes.setup: Labor violations are being listed
jokes.punchline: Burns' casual admission that he enslaved plane crash survivors
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation of Burns' evil. The casual tone makes the horrific revelation even funnier. Great character integration.

dialogue.speaker: Agent Wesson
dialogue.line: You can't just enslave people who survive plane crashes.

dialogue.speaker: Mr. Burns
dialogue.line: Well, technically, I can. And I did. Twice.

jokes.text: Get back to work, Stewart! [Quacking]
jokes.setup: Burns mentioned his hiring policies
jokes.punchline: He's apparently employing a duck named Stewart
jokes.explanation: Perfect absurdist moment. The casual treatment of a duck employee is wonderfully bizarre and perfectly economical.

dialogue.speaker: Mr. Burns
dialogue.line: A duck? Named Stewart? Preposterous! Though I suppose he does work for minimum wage and doesn't complain about the working conditions.

jokes.text: Menachem Begin wore a pair just like them.
jokes.setup: Bart gets thick glasses for his lazy eye
jokes.punchline: Doctor compares him to former Israeli Prime Minister
jokes.explanation: Oddly specific reference that's funny for its randomness and the visual it creates.

dialogue.speaker: Eye Doctor
dialogue.line: Well, your son has a severe lazy eye. I'm afraid he's gonna need thick corrective lenses.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: How thick?

dialogue.speaker: Eye Doctor
dialogue.line: Well, he'll look like Menachem Begin.

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: Who's Menachem Begin?

dialogue.speaker: Eye Doctor
dialogue.line: An Israeli Prime Minister. Very powerful man. Feared throughout the Middle East.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Cool!

jokes.text: My voice is crazy with this spraying already. Oy, I feel so much better, Mr. Medical Science-type Person.
jokes.setup: Bart gets throat spray treatment
jokes.punchline: The spray makes him speak with exaggerated Jewish inflection
jokes.explanation: Voice change gag that plays on ethnic stereotypes. The 'Medical Science-type Person' adds to the affected speech pattern.

dialogue.speaker: Doctor
dialogue.line: This throat spray will help with your voice.

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: Thanks, Doc!

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: Oy vey, what's happening to my voice? I'm talking like I'm from Brooklyn or something!

jokes.text: And we can't take off our pants when it gets real hot. And we won't be able to pee in the drinking fountain.
jokes.setup: Workers complain about having to work with women
jokes.punchline: Their complaints reveal disgusting workplace behavior
jokes.explanation: Escalating grossness that satirizes workplace misogyny by making the men's behavior obviously repulsive.

dialogue.speaker: Plant Worker 1
dialogue.line: I don't know why they had to hire women at the plant. It's just not right.

dialogue.speaker: Plant Worker 2
dialogue.line: Yeah, now we can't scratch ourselves or tell dirty jokes anymore.

dialogue.speaker: Plant Worker 3
dialogue.line: And I had to stop using the women's bathroom to take naps on the floor.

dialogue.speaker: Plant Worker 1
dialogue.line: This is the worst day of my life.

jokes.text: Yeah-- I mean, that-- You know, if we wanted to. Not that I ever did.
jokes.setup: Worker mentions peeing in drinking fountain
jokes.punchline: His awkward backtracking reveals he probably did it
jokes.explanation: Classic 'protesting too much' moment. The awkward backpedaling makes it funnier.

dialogue.speaker: Plant Worker
dialogue.line: I didn't pee in the drinking fountain.

dialogue.speaker: Plant Worker
dialogue.line: Well, not today anyway.

dialogue.speaker: Plant Worker
dialogue.line: I mean, not since last Tuesday.

dialogue.speaker: Plant Worker
dialogue.line: Look, the point is, I'm not doing it anymore.

jokes.text: Ain't you never seen a naked chick ridin' a clam before?
jokes.setup: Homer reacts strongly to seeing Mindy
jokes.punchline: Worker's crude explanation for Homer's reaction
jokes.explanation: Crude workplace humor that misinterprets Homer's attraction as just being shocked by nudity.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Whoa! Mindy!

dialogue.speaker: Plant Worker
dialogue.line: What's wrong with him?

dialogue.speaker: Plant Worker
dialogue.line: His brain's telling him one thing, but his body's telling him something completely different.

jokes.text: I probably shouldn't have eaten that packet of powdered gravy I found in the parking lot.
jokes.setup: Homer runs away after seeing Mindy, workers wonder what happened
jokes.punchline: Homer blames his reaction on eating parking lot gravy
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer logic - blaming an emotional reaction on his disgusting eating habits. Great character integration and misdirection.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: It wasn't my fault! I ate some gravy I found in the parking lot!

jokes.text: Yeah, nice shoes, uh, two feet. Yeah.
jokes.setup: Bullies mock Bart's glasses with 'Nice glasses, four eyes'
jokes.punchline: Jimbo's failed attempt at an equivalent insult about shoes
jokes.explanation: Brilliant subversion of bully tropes. Jimbo's stupidity shines through his failed attempt to create an insult.

dialogue.speaker: Jimbo
dialogue.line: Nice shoes, four feet!

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: That doesn't even make sense.

jokes.text: Your appearance is comical to me.
jokes.setup: Following the failed insult attempts
jokes.punchline: Nelson's overly formal, clinical description of mockery
jokes.explanation: Perfect Nelson line - unexpectedly articulate and formal way to say 'you look funny.' The clinical tone makes it funnier.

dialogue.speaker: Nelson
dialogue.line: Ah yes, the practice of derisive commentary coupled with physical gestures of contempt. A time-honored tradition of peer-to-peer psychological warfare, if you will. Ha-ha!

jokes.text: [Gasps] I'm a nerd! [Gasps] So am I!
jokes.setup: Bart realizes his appearance makes him look nerdy
jokes.punchline: Milhouse's identical realization about himself
jokes.explanation: Milhouse's slower realization adds to the joke. Good parallel structure between the characters.

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: Wait a minute. With my spiky hair, my shorts, and my big eyes, I look like a total nerd!

dialogue.speaker: Milhouse
dialogue.line: Yeah, well look at me! I've got the thick glasses, the pocket protector, the high-water pants... I AM a nerd!

jokes.text: [Fish] Homer loves Mindy! Homer loves Mindy!
jokes.setup: Homer is trying to avoid thinking about Mindy
jokes.punchline: Even the fish on his dashboard are taunting him about his attraction
jokes.explanation: Surreal visual gag showing Homer's guilt manifesting as hallucinations. The singing fish is a perfect absurdist touch.

dialogue.speaker: Fish
dialogue.line: Oh, Mindy!

dialogue.speaker: Fish
dialogue.line: Kiss me, Homer!

jokes.text: Hey, Homer, you're hallucinating again. Not a good sign.
jokes.setup: Homer hallucinates singing fish
jokes.punchline: Barney, a notorious alcoholic, warns Homer about hallucinations
jokes.explanation: Perfect irony - Barney giving advice about hallucinations. Great character integration with his drinking problem.

dialogue.speaker: Barney
dialogue.line: Homer, those fish ain't real. I know hallucinations when I see 'em. I've had more hallucinations than you've had donuts. And that's saying something, pal.

jokes.text: Yeah. Usually you just take the box of doughnuts into the bathroom.
jokes.setup: Homer is acting unusually talkative during coffee break
jokes.punchline: Revelation of Homer's disgusting normal bathroom habits
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer characterization - his gross eating habits revealed casually. The matter-of-fact delivery makes it funnier.

dialogue.speaker: Carl
dialogue.line: Dude, why are you being so chatty today?

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: I don't know, I just feel like talking!

dialogue.speaker: Carl
dialogue.line: Well, cut it out. You're freaking me out.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Come on, we're friends! We should share things.

dialogue.speaker: Carl
dialogue.line: We are NOT friends, Homer.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Oh, we're not? Well, I go through a lot of stuff you wouldn't believe. Just this morning, I had the most incredible—

dialogue.speaker: Carl
dialogue.line: Stop. I don't want to hear about your bathroom habits.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: How did you know I was going to—

dialogue.speaker: Carl
dialogue.line: Because you always do this.

jokes.text: See, I got this friend named Joey Jo-Jo Junior Shabadoo. That's the worst name I ever heard. [Sobbing] Bye, Joey Jo-Jo!
jokes.setup: Homer pretends to ask about a 'friend' with a ridiculous name
jokes.punchline: The name is so bad it makes the actual person with that name leave in tears
jokes.explanation: One of the series' best jokes. The absurd name, Moe's brutal honesty, and the reveal that someone actually has that name creates perfect comedy escalation.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Hey Moe, I got a question about a friend of mine.

dialogue.speaker: Moe
dialogue.line: Yeah, what is it?

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: His name is Joey Jo-Jo Junior Shabadoo.

dialogue.speaker: Moe
dialogue.line: Joey Jo-Jo? JOEY JO-JO!

dialogue.speaker: Joey Jo-Jo
dialogue.line: That's me!

dialogue.speaker: Moe
dialogue.line: Get out! And take that stupid name with you!

dialogue.speaker: Joey Jo-Jo
dialogue.line: Why does everyone always do this to me?
Joey Jo-Jo runs out of the bar in tears.

jokes.text: [ Sobbing ] Bye, JoeyJo-Jo!
jokes.setup: Moe insulting the fake name
jokes.punchline: Someone in bar is actually upset by the insult
jokes.explanation: Brilliant unexpected twist - someone actually HAS that terrible name. The timing and sob are perfect. One of the series' best sight gags.

dialogue.speaker: Moe
dialogue.line: Hey, what's the matter with you? You look like you got hit by a truck!

dialogue.speaker: Barney
dialogue.line: I did.

dialogue.speaker: Moe
dialogue.line: Well, that explains it.

jokes.text: It was on one of these bar napkins.
jokes.setup: Homer compliments Barney's insightful relationship advice
jokes.punchline: Barney admits he just read it off a bar napkin
jokes.explanation: Perfect deflation of seemingly wise advice. Great character moment for Barney - honest about his limitations.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Barney, that was some really insightful relationship advice.

dialogue.speaker: Barney
dialogue.line: Thanks, Homer. I just read it off this bar napkin.

jokes.text: Hey, my favorite-- raspberry swirl with a double glaze! Double glaze. [Gurgling]
jokes.setup: Homer and Mindy discover they like the same donut
jokes.punchline: Homer's orgasmic reaction to hearing about double glaze
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer food obsession moment. His sexual response to food descriptions is perfectly in character.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Mmmmm... double glaze.

dialogue.speaker: Mindy
dialogue.line: You like double glaze too?

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Oh yeah... oh yeah... ohhhhhhh yeah!

jokes.text: Foul temptress. I'll bet she thinks Ziggy's gotten too preachy too.
jokes.setup: Homer discovers more things in common with Mindy
jokes.punchline: He imagines they'd even agree about comic strip criticism
jokes.explanation: Brilliant specific detail about Ziggy becoming 'too preachy' - perfectly captures how people project compatibility onto crushes.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Mindy, we have so much in common. We probably even agree about comic strips.

dialogue.speaker: Mindy
dialogue.line: Oh, I hate Garfield.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Me too! It's not funny, and the cat's a jerk.

dialogue.speaker: Mindy
dialogue.line: I know! And I hate Cathy too.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Me too! What's the deal with her outfit?

jokes.text: I'll just push the button for the stimulator-- I mean, elevator.
jokes.setup: Homer and Mindy are alone in elevator
jokes.punchline: Homer's Freudian slip reveals his sexual thoughts
jokes.explanation: Classic Freudian slip joke that reveals Homer's state of mind. The correction makes it more obvious.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: So Mindy, I've been meaning to ask you... would you like to get some coffee sometime? I mean, you know, just as friends. I think you're a really intelligent woman and I respect your... your...

dialogue.speaker: Mindy
dialogue.line: My what, Homer?

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Your... breasts! I mean, your brains! Your brains!

jokes.text: Think unsexy thoughts. Think unsexy thoughts. Think unsexy thoughts. Oh, that's unsexy.
jokes.setup: Homer tries to control his attraction by thinking unsexy thoughts
jokes.punchline: He apparently looks at Mindy and finds her unsexy, then immediately contradicts himself
jokes.explanation: Homer's failed attempt at self-control. The visual timing would be crucial to this joke's success.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Mindy, you're a beautiful woman. I'm going to think of you as something unsexy. You're a beer-guzzling accountant.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Nope, still got it.

jokes.text: [Humming Theme From I Dream of Jeannie] Ew! [Burps] D'oh!
jokes.setup: Homer continues trying to think unsexy thoughts
jokes.punchline: His own burp disgusts him, followed by his signature exclamation
jokes.explanation: Homer's gross bodily functions helping him stay faithful. The I Dream of Jeannie reference adds randomness.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: His own burp disgusts him

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: D'oh!

jokes.text: They're burned on the outside, but they're frozen on the inside, so it balances out.
jokes.setup: Lisa made fish sticks for dinner
jokes.punchline: Her logical explanation for completely botched cooking
jokes.explanation: Perfect Lisa logic - applying intellectual reasoning to complete cooking failure. The 'balance' concept is brilliantly absurd.

dialogue.speaker: Lisa
dialogue.line: Well, according to my calculations, the fish sticks should have been perfectly cooked at 350 degrees for 12 minutes. The fact that they're now charcoal briquettes can only mean one thing: the oven is operating in some sort of alternate dimension where time moves differently.

jokes.text: That raccoon stole my lamb chop!
jokes.setup: Family dinner scene
jokes.punchline: A raccoon apparently steals food from baby Maggie
jokes.explanation: Random visual gag that adds to the household chaos. The casual treatment of wildlife intrusion is funny.

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: Maggie, eat your vegetables.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Hey, where'd the baby's food go?

dialogue.speaker: Lisa
dialogue.line: A raccoon! It's stealing from Maggie!

dialogue.speaker: Maggie
dialogue.line: *pacifier suck*

jokes.text: That raccoon stole my lamb chop!
jokes.setup: Family dinner scene
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of wildlife stealing food at dinner table
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist family dysfunction - treating bizarre occurrence as normal

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: Well, I spent all afternoon preparing this roast beef dinner.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Mmm, roast beef.

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: I had to fight off three raccoons just to get the groceries inside.

dialogue.speaker: Lisa
dialogue.line: That's actually a common problem in suburban areas. Raccoons are highly intelligent and have learned to associate human dwellings with food sources.

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: Cool! Ninja trash pandas!

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: The only thing stealing food at my dinner table is me!

jokes.text: I got two dollars off because mine got smeared.
jokes.setup: Marge tells Homer about getting their pictures on T-shirts
jokes.punchline: She got a discount because her shirt was defective
jokes.explanation: Subtle Marge humor - finding value in defective merchandise. Shows her practical, bargain-hunting nature.

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: Homer, I have wonderful news! They're putting our picture on T-shirts!

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: That's great, Marge!

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: And I got a wonderful discount because my shirt was defective.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Defective? What's wrong with it?

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: It has you on it.

jokes.text: Did fooling around on their wives make them great? We'll find out next when we play ''Hail to the Cheat.''
jokes.setup: TV show about presidential affairs
jokes.punchline: Pun on 'Hail to the Chief' with cheating theme
jokes.explanation: Decent pun that ties to Homer's situation. The presidential theme makes the wordplay work.

dialogue.speaker: TV Announcer
dialogue.line: Coming next on Fox: a bold new drama about presidential affairs. 'Hale to the Thief.'

jokes.text: The Burmese melon fly has over 1000 sex partners and suffers virtually no guilt.
jokes.setup: Homer is watching TV while struggling with guilt about attraction
jokes.punchline: The nature documentary perfectly targets his emotional state
jokes.explanation: Perfect situational irony - the TV seems to be mocking Homer's moral struggle. The specific number '1000' makes it funnier.

dialogue.speaker: Nature Documentary Narrator
dialogue.line: In the springtime, the male of the species experiences an uncontrollable urge...

dialogue.speaker: Homer Simpson
dialogue.line: Oh, that's me!

dialogue.speaker: Nature Documentary Narrator
dialogue.line: ...to seek out a new mate, even though he already has one at home.

dialogue.speaker: Homer Simpson
dialogue.line: D'oh!

jokes.text: Homer Simpson, that's a dilly of a pickle. Hey, let's conference you with Marge, huh?
jokes.setup: Homer calls a hotline about temptation
jokes.punchline: The operator suggests the worst possible solution
jokes.explanation: The cheerful 'dilly of a pickle' contrasted with the terrible advice creates good comedy. Homer's panic is justified.

dialogue.speaker: Homer Simpson
dialogue.line: Hello, is this the temptation hotline?

dialogue.speaker: Hotline Operator
dialogue.line: Yes, sir. What seems to be troubling you?

dialogue.speaker: Homer Simpson
dialogue.line: Well, there's this donut right in front of me, and I'm trying not to eat it.

dialogue.speaker: Hotline Operator
dialogue.line: I see. My advice is to eat the donut.

dialogue.speaker: Homer Simpson
dialogue.line: You're a lifesaver!

jokes.text: Sir Isa-who? Oh, very well. [Gasps] Colonel Klink! Did you ever get my letters?
jokes.setup: Guardian angel appears as Sir Isaac Newton
jokes.punchline: Homer doesn't recognize the great scientist but knows a sitcom character
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer characterization - ignorant of science but obsessed with TV. The personal investment in Klink is perfectly absurd.

dialogue.speaker: Guardian Angel
dialogue.line: Homer, I am Sir Isaac Newton, discoverer of gravity.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Oh, you're that guy from that sci-fi show... Red Dwarf!

jokes.text: Did you know Hogan had tunnels all over your camp?
jokes.setup: Angel tries to focus Homer on his moral dilemma
jokes.punchline: Homer is more interested in spoiling Hogan's Heroes plots
jokes.explanation: Homer's priorities are completely wrong - more concerned with TV trivia than his marriage crisis. Perfect character moment.

dialogue.speaker: Angel
dialogue.line: Homer, you're facing a serious moral dilemma. You need to focus on what's really important.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: You're right, you're right. Wait, did you see Hogan's Heroes last night? Sergeant Schultz almost caught Colonel Hogan!

dialogue.speaker: Angel
dialogue.line: Homer, that's not what I meant—

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: No wait, don't tell me what happens! Well, actually, go ahead.

jokes.text: Are you happy, Jeeves? Yes, sir. Quite.
jokes.setup: Guardian angel shows Homer an alternate reality where he's rich
jokes.punchline: Even in his fantasy, Homer checks on his servant's happiness
jokes.explanation: Sweet character moment - even in wealth, Homer cares about others' feelings. Shows his fundamental decency.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Jeeves, are you happy?

dialogue.speaker: Jeeves
dialogue.line: Yes, sir. Very much so.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Good. Now get me a donut.

jokes.text: [Gasps] Marge lives here? Madam President, your approval rating is soaring.
jokes.setup: Homer sees Marge in the alternate timeline
jokes.punchline: She's become President of the United States
jokes.explanation: Great escalation showing how much better Marge's life became without Homer. The President reveal is perfectly absurd.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Marge? You're President of the United States?

dialogue.speaker: President Marge
dialogue.line: Yes, Homer. I've worked very hard to get here.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Well, I'm very proud of you. Now where's my beer?

jokes.text: The refuge of the damned. A place where we can work on our extra-credit assignments without fear of reprisal.
jokes.setup: Bart discovers where the nerds hide
jokes.punchline: They describe homework as their sanctuary activity
jokes.explanation: Perfect nerd characterization - treating academic work as both refuge and rebellion. The formal language adds to the humor.

dialogue.speaker: Nerd Leader
dialogue.line: Welcome to our secret hideout, Bart. Here we can be ourselves.

dialogue.speaker: Nerd Leader
dialogue.line: We do our homework here.

dialogue.speaker: Bart Simpson
dialogue.line: Homework? That's your idea of fun?

dialogue.speaker: Nerd Leader
dialogue.line: It's more than fun, Bart. Homework is our sanctuary activity.

jokes.text: [Kids] Excelsior!
jokes.setup: Nerds welcome Bart to their group
jokes.punchline: Their rallying cry is an archaic exclamation meaning 'ever upward'
jokes.explanation: Perfect nerd battle cry - pretentious and academic. Single word packs a lot of character information.

dialogue.speaker: Nerd Kid 1
dialogue.line: Welcome to our group, Bart!

dialogue.speaker: Nerd Kids
dialogue.line: Ay caramba!

jokes.text: Oh, Mindy, you came and you gave without flaking, But I sent you Ben Gay
jokes.setup: Homer singing about his feelings for Mindy
jokes.punchline: Mangling 'Mandy' song lyrics with pharmaceutical reference
jokes.explanation: Clever parody showing Homer's confusion of romantic and mundane thoughts

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Oh, Mindy, why does love gotta be so hard? Why can't it just be easy like... like...

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Candy... I'm half crazy all for the love of Candy!

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Wait, no... Candy... with a shot of lithium...

jokes.text: Because I have a small role in a Broadway musical. It's not much, but it's a start.
jokes.setup: Lisa asks Homer why he's singing
jokes.punchline: Homer's elaborate lie about being on Broadway
jokes.explanation: Homer's quick thinking creates an absurdly elaborate cover story. The false modesty ('it's not much') adds to the lie's ridiculousness.

dialogue.speaker: Lisa
dialogue.line: Dad, why are you singing?

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Well, sweetheart, I was on Broadway! Yes, I was the star of a show called 'Oh, the Humanities!' It was a smash hit. We ran for eight weeks. I won a Tony Award, which I then lost in a poker game to Bette Midler.

jokes.text: Judging from your song, you're infatuated with a woman named Mindy or a man named Andy.
jokes.setup: Lisa analyzes Homer's song lyrics
jokes.punchline: Her logical deduction includes the possibility he's attracted to a man
jokes.explanation: Lisa's analytical mind creates unexpected comedy. Her matter-of-fact inclusion of homosexuality shows her progressive thinking.

dialogue.speaker: Lisa
dialogue.line: Well, based on these lyrics, Dad could be attracted to a woman, or possibly a man.

jokes.text: Sucker!
jokes.setup: Homer desperately trying to distract Lisa
jokes.punchline: Childish victory when distraction works
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective. Homer's gleeful 'sucker!' captures his childish side perfectly. Great economy.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Look Lisa, a distraction!

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: D'oh!

jokes.text: Oh, no, I'm sweating like Roger Ebert.
jokes.setup: Homer is nervous about talking to Mindy
jokes.punchline: His comparison to the film critic's perspiration
jokes.explanation: Brilliant specific cultural reference. Ebert was known for sweating during TV appearances. Perfect economy in the comparison.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: I'm so nervous about talking to Mindy. What if I say something stupid?

dialogue.speaker: Lenny
dialogue.line: You always say something stupid, Homer.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Yeah, but not on purpose! Look at me, I'm sweating like that film critic guy.

dialogue.speaker: Barney
dialogue.line: Which one?

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: You know, the one who's always sweating. Gene Siskel!

jokes.text: Wow! Capital City! The Windy Apple!
jokes.setup: Burns announces they're going to Capital City
jokes.punchline: Mindy combines Chicago's 'Windy City' with New York's 'Big Apple' nickname
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay mixing city nicknames. Shows The Simpsons' fictional geography through character enthusiasm.

dialogue.speaker: Mr. Burns
dialogue.line: We're going to Capital City.

dialogue.speaker: Mindy
dialogue.line: Oh, so it's like Chicago's 'Windy City' mixed with New York's 'Big Apple'... the Big Windy?

jokes.text: Did you know Kinch had a radio in the coffee pot? He did? Mmm!
jokes.setup: Homer still focused on Hogan's Heroes trivia
jokes.punchline: Even the angel/Klink is interested in the show details
jokes.explanation: Good continuation of the Hogan's Heroes obsession. Even divine intervention gets sidetracked by TV trivia.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Colonel Klink would never fall for that!

dialogue.speaker: Angel/Klink
dialogue.line: You know, I always wondered how Hogan managed to pull off that escape in Season 3, Episode 12.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Right?! It's genius!

jokes.text: [Wolf Whistle, Meowing] [Imitates Bed Squeaking, Growls, Pants] [Barking, Howling] [Gibberish] Hubba-hubba.
jokes.setup: Bellhop shows Homer and Mindy to their hotel rooms
jokes.punchline: His elaborate sound effects suggesting sexual activity
jokes.explanation: Crude humor that relies more on sound effects than wit. The extended nature makes it feel forced rather than funny.

dialogue.speaker: Bellhop
dialogue.line: Here are your rooms. Enjoy your stay.

dialogue.speaker: Bellhop
dialogue.line: *makes exaggerated moaning and rhythmic sounds*

jokes.text: All I'm gonna use this bed for is sleeping, eating and maybe building a little fort. That's it!
jokes.setup: Homer defending his moral intentions
jokes.punchline: Innocent activities including childish fort-building
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer - the fort detail reveals his childlike innocence even in tempting situation. Shows his essential character.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: I'm not a bad guy! I do innocent things all the time. Why, just last weekend I built a fort out of couch cushions.

jokes.text: No, Homer. Let's do it. Let's call room service!
jokes.setup: Mindy suggests they do something 'wicked' that could get them in trouble
jokes.punchline: She means ordering expensive room service, not sex
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection that subverts both Homer's and the audience's expectations about what the 'wicked idea' would be.

dialogue.speaker: Mindy
dialogue.line: Come on, Homer. Let's do something wicked. Something that could get us in real trouble.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Wicked? You mean...?

dialogue.speaker: Mindy
dialogue.line: I'm talking about ordering the expensive room service. The stuff they don't want you to order.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Oh.

jokes.text: Fly, my pretties. Fly!
jokes.setup: Burns sending surveillance to track room service charges
jokes.punchline: Wizard of Oz reference with flying monkeys
jokes.explanation: Classic Burns - treating employees like the Wicked Witch. The reference fits his theatrical management style.

dialogue.speaker: Mr. Burns
dialogue.line: Smithers, I want you to install surveillance cameras in every room. I suspect someone is ordering room service without my permission.

dialogue.speaker: Smithers
dialogue.line: Very good, sir. I'll have them operational by tonight.

dialogue.speaker: Mr. Burns
dialogue.line: Excellent. And if I see one unauthorized BLT, there will be hell to pay. They'll be flying monkeys compared to what I'll do to the culprit.

jokes.text: [Both] Mmm. Foot-long chili dog. [Both Gasp]
jokes.setup: Homer and Mindy reach for the same food under the bed
jokes.punchline: They accidentally touch hands reaching for a phallic-shaped food item
jokes.explanation: Obvious sexual metaphor with the hot dog shape. The simultaneous reaching creates the romantic comedy moment but feels predictable.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Mmm... donuts...

dialogue.speaker: Mindy
dialogue.line: Oh!

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Whoa!

dialogue.speaker: Mindy
dialogue.line: Sorry, I didn't see you there.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: No problem. We can share.

jokes.text: [Marge's Voice: Mechanical Humming Sound] [Screams] It's a sign!
jokes.setup: Homer and Mindy are about to kiss
jokes.punchline: The hotel ice machine sounds like Marge, which Homer interprets as divine intervention
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist moment where guilt manifests as supernatural intervention. Homer's interpretation of machinery as a sign is perfectly absurd.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Wait, do you hear that? It sounds like Marge saying 'stop'!

dialogue.speaker: Mindy
dialogue.line: That's just the ice machine.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: It's a sign from above! Even the ice machine knows this is wrong!

jokes.text: Kids, did anyone pray for giant shoes? I did! Okilly-dokilly.
jokes.setup: Bart's orthopedic shoes have arrived
jokes.punchline: Ned's cheerful admission that he prayed for this bizarre outcome
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ned character moment - his religious devotion extends to praying for neighbor's medical equipment. The 'okilly-dokilly' catchphrase adds charm.

dialogue.speaker: Ned Flanders
dialogue.line: Well, I'll be diddly-doodly! Those orthopedic shoes are a blessing from the Lord! I prayed every night that Bart would need corrective footwear!

jokes.text: Gentlemen, the nerd you knew is dead. Beat me, and you will be beating one of your own.
jokes.setup: Bart confronting bullies
jokes.punchline: Overly dramatic speech about nerd transformation
jokes.explanation: Good dramatic speech parody. Bart treating nerd acceptance as heroic transformation works well.

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: Listen up, you guys. I know you think nerds are losers, but let me tell you something. One day you're gonna need that nerd. You're gonna need him to fix your computer, or help you with your taxes, or figure out why your internet is down. And when that day comes, you're gonna wish you treated him with respect.

jokes.text: Gentlemen, the nerd you knew is dead. Beat me, and you will be beating one of your own.
jokes.setup: Bullies approach Bart for his usual beating
jokes.punchline: Bart reveals his transformation into a nerd changes the social dynamics
jokes.explanation: Smart commentary on social hierarchies. Bart's dramatic declaration and the bullies' confused response creates good comedy.

dialogue.speaker: Kearney
dialogue.line: Hey, Simpson! Time for your daily beating!

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: Actually, I've decided to embrace my inner nerd. I'm starting a Linux user group and I've memorized all the periodic table elements.

dialogue.speaker: Dolph
dialogue.line: Uh... what?

dialogue.speaker: Jimbo
dialogue.line: Did he just say something about... computers?

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: Furthermore, I'm organizing a Dungeons and Dragons campaign and I have opinions about which Star Trek series is superior.

dialogue.speaker: Kearney
dialogue.line: We don't know what he's talking about, but it sounds like nerd stuff.

dialogue.speaker: Dolph
dialogue.line: Yeah, and now we gotta respect him or something.

dialogue.speaker: Jimbo
dialogue.line: Come on, guys. We can't beat up a nerd. That's like, totally uncool.

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: See? The social hierarchy has inverted. I'm now untouchable.

jokes.text: Thanks for poisoning the planet, bastard! Get bent! No more Chernobyls! Go to hell!
jokes.setup: Homer and Mindy attend the energy convention
jokes.punchline: Protesters hurl both environmental concerns and crude insults
jokes.explanation: Generic protest humor that mixes legitimate concerns with random hostility. Not particularly clever or original.

dialogue.speaker: Protester 1
dialogue.line: Stop the nuclear power plant!

dialogue.speaker: Protester 2
dialogue.line: You're destroying the environment!

dialogue.speaker: Protester 3
dialogue.line: Hey fatso, go back to the donut shop!

dialogue.speaker: Protester 4
dialogue.line: Nuclear power kills!

jokes.text: Hey, we're out of these 'new love' cookies. Well, open up the 'stick with your wife' barrel.
jokes.setup: Homer gets a fortune cookie about finding new love
jokes.punchline: The restaurant worker reveals they have targeted fortune cookies
jokes.explanation: Brilliant subversion of fortune cookie mysticism. The casual way the worker discusses cookie categories is perfectly absurd and memorable.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Wow, a fortune cookie about finding new love! This is amazing!

dialogue.speaker: Restaurant Worker
dialogue.line: Yeah, about that... those aren't random fortunes.

dialogue.speaker: Restaurant Worker
dialogue.line: We've got targeted fortune cookies now. Yours was selected based on your browser history and purchasing data.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: You can do that?!

dialogue.speaker: Restaurant Worker
dialogue.line: It's the future, buddy.

jokes.text: ''You will find happiness with a new love.'' Oh, even the Chinese are against me.
jokes.setup: Homer reading fortune cookie
jokes.punchline: Interprets fortune as conspiracy against his marriage
jokes.explanation: Great Homer paranoia - treating fortune cookie as personal attack. The idea of Chinese people conspiring against his marriage is wonderfully absurd.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: "You will soon be hungry again." Hey, that's not a fortune, that's a threat!

jokes.text: Well, not the boy. He drives me nuts. Sometimes I'd just like to--
jokes.setup: Homer talks about thinking of Marge and the kids
jokes.punchline: He admits Bart annoys him and suggests violent thoughts
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer parenting moment - his honest frustration with Bart even in a romantic crisis. Dark but relatable humor.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: I think about Marge and the kids all the time.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Well, except Bart. That kid really annoys me.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Sometimes I have violent thoughts about him.

jokes.text: Hey, there's a turkey behind the bed! Mmm. Marge.
jokes.setup: Homer chooses Marge over Mindy and returns home
jokes.punchline: He finds the same turkey from the hotel, showing Marge's thoughtfulness
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback that shows Marge's understanding and love. The turkey becomes a symbol of their relationship and shared simple pleasures.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Marge, I'm home! I chose you over Mindy!

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: Oh Homer, welcome back. I have a surprise for you.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: What is it?

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: It's that turkey from the hotel. I thought of you.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: You remembered the turkey? Marge, you're the best!

jokes.text: Oh, Margie, you came and you found me a turkey, On my vacation away from workie
jokes.setup: Homer is happy to be home with Marge
jokes.punchline: His song parodies 'Mandy' but celebrates mundane marital happiness
jokes.explanation: Sweet callback to his earlier song about Mindy, now redirected to appreciate Marge. The silly rhymes show Homer's genuine happiness.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Oh, Marge! I'm so glad to be home with you. You know what? I'm gonna sing you a song!

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: ♪ Marge, Marge, the morning after Marge... I thought our love would be so grand... But Marge, the pipes are frozen and the car won't start, and I'm eating cold leftover clams... ♪

tmdb_overview: Homer struggles with his attraction to new coworker Mindy, a beautiful engineer whose love for doughnuts is equal to his own.
insight: Observational humor drives 73 jokes in 22 minutes—almost one every 18 seconds.
